description: Russian-born Hebrew poet (1873-1934), widely regarded as Israel's national poet. Among the pioneers of modern Hebrew poetry; his manuscript drafts and personal letters are a primary source of early-20th-century handwritten modern Hebrew.

ingest.agent_notes: Seed writer for v0 ingest. First per-letter crops drawn from a single manuscript page (commons__bialik_el_hazippor__p0001) to validate the manual-extraction pipeline end-to-end.

references.citation: Wikimedia Commons: manuscript draft of 'El Hatzippor' (autograph).

description: Ukrainian-born Hebrew poet (1890-1931), known as 'Rachel the Poetess'. One of the most beloved poets of the Hebrew literary renaissance; her clear, lyrical cursive manuscripts are among the most legible surviving examples of early-20th-century handwritten modern Hebrew.

ingest.agent_notes: Second writer added to corpus (C03). Four upstream PDM-1.0 scans available; primary letter crops drawn from commons__rachel_gan_naul__p0001 (5184x3456, highest-resolution scan in the dataset).

references.citation: Wikimedia Commons: 'Gan Naul' manuscript photograph (Gnazim Institute archive, 2019).
